Troubleshooting

Welcome to Trigr

Trigr is a visual hotkey, macro and text expansion manager for Windows. No coding required — click a key, assign an action, done.

⌨

Hotkeys & Macros

Assign Ctrl+E or Ctrl+Alt+Q to type text, open apps, run sequences and more.

✦

Text Expansions

Type a trigger word, press Space, and it instantly expands into full formatted text.

⬡

App Profiles

Profiles auto-switch when you change windows — different macros per application.

◈

Macro Sequences

Chain actions together — type, press keys, wait for clicks, open URLs.

👤

Global Variables

Store your name, email, phone once — auto-filled silently in any expansion.

⏸

Global Pause

Instantly pause all hotkeys and expansions with one key combo. Press again to resume.

Quick start

  1. Click the Trigr icon in the system tray (bottom right of taskbar) to open the app

  2. Click Ctrl in the modifier bar above the keyboard

  3. Click any key — e.g. E — to select the Ctrl+E combination

  4. Choose an action type in the right panel and fill in the details

  5. Click Assign to Key — the key gains an amber dot

  6. Press Ctrl+E in any application — your macro fires!

System tray

Closing the Trigr window does not stop it — it keeps running in the background. Click the tray icon to reopen. The status bar shows Macros Active with a green dot when running.

The Interface

Trigr has four main areas: the title bar with profiles, the assignments sidebar, the keyboard canvas, and the action panel.

Title bar — profiles

Profile tabs across the top. Active profile highlighted in amber. Linked-app profiles show a coloured dot. Click + to add a new profile. Double-click to rename, right-click for more options.

Assignments sidebar

Left panel shows all assignments grouped by modifier layer. Click any item to jump to editing it. The sidebar auto-resets to "All" when you switch profiles.

Modifier bar

Toggle Ctrl Alt Shift Win to select the hotkey layer. The keyboard is dimmed until at least one modifier is active.

Action panel

Opens on the right when you click a key. Shows the action type grid and all form fields for that assignment.

Status bar

Bottom bar shows macros active state, assignment count, and the Listener/Executor engine indicators — both should show green when the engine is running.

Hotkeys

A hotkey is a modifier+key combination that triggers an action. Press Ctrl+E and your email signature types itself. Press Alt+Backquote and your CAD app launches.

Modifier combinations

Modifier(s)ExampleCommon use
CtrlCtrl+EType text, open URLs
AltAlt+BackquoteOpen specific applications
ShiftShift+F5Run macro sequences
WinWin+EOpen folders
Ctrl+AltCtrl+Alt+QComplex CAD commands
Ctrl+ShiftCtrl+Shift+EAlternative email template

Action types

✦

Type Text

Types any text at the cursor — plain or formatted.

⌨

Send Hotkey

Fires a different key combo — remap shortcuts.

⬡

Open App

Launches any application or file.

⊕

Open URL

Opens a website in your browser.

📁

Open Folder

Opens a folder in File Explorer.

◈

Macro Sequence

Chains multiple actions in order.

Creating a hotkey

Select a modifier, click a key, configure the action. Same process for all action types.

  1. Click a modifier button above the keyboard — e.g. Ctrl for the Ctrl layer, or Alt for the Alt layer

  2. Click the key to assign on the visual keyboard. Keys with an amber dot are already assigned on this layer.

  3. Choose your action type from the grid in the right panel

  4. Fill in the required fields for that action type

  5. Optionally add a Display Label — shown in the sidebar. Auto-generated if left blank.

  6. Click Assign to Key

Editing, reassigning, duplicating

Click any assigned key (amber dot) to edit it. Use Reassign in the panel header to move the assignment to a different key combo without recreating it. Use Duplicate to copy it to a new hotkey.

Type Text

Instantly types any text at your cursor when the hotkey is pressed.

  1. Select a modifier layer and click a key

  2. Select Type Text from the action grid

  3. Type your text in the replacement field. Multi-line is supported — press Enter for new lines.

  4. Click Assign to Key

CAD and specialist software

If text is not appearing correctly in an application like BricsCAD, go to Settings → Compatibility and switch to Direct Keystrokes mode. This works with most CAD programs out of the box.

Send Hotkey

Fires a different key combination when your hotkey is pressed — remap shortcuts, create easier alternatives to complex combos.

  1. Select Send Hotkey from the action grid

  2. Toggle the modifier keys to send (Ctrl, Alt, Shift, Win) using the buttons in the form

  3. Select the target key from the dropdown

  4. A preview shows the full combination — e.g. Ctrl + Shift + T

  5. Click Assign to Key

Press thisSends thisEffect
Ctrl+1Ctrl+Shift+TReopen last closed tab in browser
Ctrl+QAlt+F4Close current window

Open App / Open Folder

Launch any application, file or folder with a single hotkey.

Open App

  1. Select Open App from the action grid

  2. Click Browse and navigate to the .exe file

  3. Optionally add a Display Name — e.g. "BricsCAD"

  4. Click Assign to Key

Opening files directly

Browse to any file — not just .exe files. Trigr opens it in its default application. Useful for frequently accessed spreadsheets, PDFs or project files.

Open Folder

  1. Select Open Folder from the action grid

  2. Click Browse and select a folder

  3. Click Assign to Key

Open URL

Open any website in your default browser with a single hotkey.

  1. Select Open URL from the action grid

  2. Type or paste the full URL including https://

  3. Optionally add a display name — e.g. "Gmail"

  4. Click Assign to Key

Macro Sequence

Chain multiple actions together into a single hotkey — type text, press keys, wait for input, open URLs — executed in order.

Step typeWhat it doesExample
Type TextTypes text at the cursorpline
Press KeyPresses a key or key comboEnter
Wait (ms)Pauses for milliseconds500
Open URLOpens a URL mid-sequencehttps://...
Wait for InputPauses until user clicks or presses a keyLeft Click — Press & Release

  1. Select Macro Sequence from the action grid

  2. Click + Add Step to add your first step

  3. Choose the step type from the dropdown and enter the value

  4. Keep adding steps. Drag the ⠿ handle on the left to reorder.

  5. Click Assign to Key

CAD example — draw pline then wait for click

Step 1: Type Text → pline  ·  Step 2: Press Key → Enter  ·  Step 3: Wait for Input → Left Click  ·  Step 4: Type Text → close  ·  Step 5: Press Key → Enter

Input method for CAD

Set the input method dropdown at the top of the sequence form to Direct Keystrokes for use in BricsCAD and most engineering applications.

Mouse Buttons

Assign macros to mouse buttons — Middle Click, Back, Forward, and extra buttons on gaming mice. Switch to the Mouse view using the toggle above the keyboard.

Assigning mouse buttons

  1. Click 🖱 Mouse in the view toggle above the keyboard

  2. Optionally select a modifier (Ctrl, Alt, Shift) first to create a layered combination like Ctrl+Back

  3. Click the mouse button you want to assign in the diagram

  4. Choose your action type and configure it in the right panel

  5. Click Assign to Key

Left and Right Click

Assigning to bare Left or Right Click replaces their normal function — only do this with a modifier held, e.g. Ctrl+Left Click, or in an app-specific profile where that app handles clicks differently.

Extra buttons (X1–X5)

The X1–X5 slots correspond to the extra side buttons on gaming and productivity mice. If your mouse only has Back/Forward, the X1–X5 slots won't trigger.

Mouse button reference

ButtonStandard nameCommon use
LeftButton 1Primary click — assign with modifier only
RightButton 2Context menu — assign with modifier only
MiddleButton 3Tab close, scroll lock — safe to reassign bare
BackButton 4 / XButton1Browser back — ideal for Undo macros
ForwardButton 5 / XButton2Browser forward — ideal for Redo macros
X1–X5Extra buttonsGaming mice side buttons

Double Press

Assign a second action to any hotkey that fires when you press it twice quickly — two different actions from one key combo.

  1. Assign a Single Press action to any hotkey as normal

  2. In the action panel, scroll down to the Double Press ×2 section

  3. Choose an action type for the double tap — it can be completely different to the single press

  4. Click Update — the key shows a ×2 badge in the sidebar

Press modeWhen it firesExample
Single pressOne tap — no second tap within detection windowCtrl+E → type email sign-off
Double press ×2Two taps within ~300msCtrl+E ×2 → open Gmail in browser
×2 badge

Assignments with a double press action show a ×2 badge in the Assignments sidebar so you can see at a glance which keys have dual actions.

Numpad

Expand the keyboard to reveal the numpad and navigation cluster — all fully assignable like any other key.

Click the ▶ Numpad toggle at the right edge of the keyboard area to slide the numpad panel open or closed.

SectionKeys
Navigation clusterPrtSc, Scr Lk, Pause, Insert, Delete, Home, End, PgUp, PgDn
Numpad numbersNum Lock, 0–9, Decimal (.)
Numpad operatorsNum +, Num −, Num ×, Num ÷, Num Enter
Full-size keyboards

Ideal for assigning extra macros to the numpad without touching the main layout. Bare key assignments on numpad keys are available in app-specific profiles.

Text Expansions

Type a short trigger word, press Space, and Trigr instantly replaces it with your full text — anywhere on your computer.

How it works

Type your trigger word anywhere — email, Word, browser, CAD — then press Space. Trigr deletes the trigger word and pastes your replacement text instantly.

Text ExpansionHotkey (Type Text)
Trigger: type a word + SpaceTrigger: press a key combo
Rich text with formattingPlain or rich text
Dynamic fields — dates, clipboardStatic text only
Global across all profilesPer-profile

Creating a text expansion

Expansions are created from the Text Expansions panel.

📷
Screenshot
SCREENSHOT 10
Creating an expansion — trigger field with "signoff" entered, rich text editor with sign-off content, category dropdown, Save button

  1. In the Text Expansions panel, click + Add

  2. Enter your trigger word — short, no spaces. Examples: signoff, myemail, /me

  3. Type your replacement text in the editor. Use the toolbar (B, I, list buttons) for formatting.

  4. Optionally assign a Category using the dropdown

  5. Click Save

Choose unique trigger words

Avoid common words. me would fire every time you type "me" in conversation. Use a prefix like /me or myemail.

Dynamic fields

Click Insert in the toolbar to add automatically-generated content — dates, clipboard, fill-in prompts.

FieldInsertsExample
ClipboardCurrent clipboard contentsWhatever you last copied
DD/MM/YYYYToday's date (UK format)22/03/2026
MM/DD/YYYYToday's date (US format)03/22/2026
YYYY-MM-DDToday's date (ISO)2026-03-22
HH:MMCurrent time14:35
HH:MM:SSCurrent time with seconds14:35:42
DayDay of the weekMonday
↕ CursorCursor position after expansionCursor moves here
Fill-in: LabelPrompts for input at expansion timePopup: "Enter Recipient Name"
Fill-in fields

When an expansion with a fill-in field fires, a small popup asks you to type a value before pasting. The typed value replaces the field in the output. Ideal for email templates where the recipient name changes.

Categories

Organise expansions into named, colour-coded tabs — Email, Legal, CAD, Support — to keep your library manageable.

  1. Click + Category next to the tab bar

  2. Type a name and press Enter

  3. When creating an expansion, use the Category dropdown to assign it

Click a category tab to filter to that group only. The All tab shows everything grouped by category.

Managing categories

Right-click any category tab to access rename, change colour, and delete options. Deleting a category moves its expansions to Uncategorised — they are never deleted.

Colour coding

Each category has a colour dot shown on its tab and on every expansion row in that category. Change it via right-click → Change Colour — the colour picker appears directly below the tab.

Reordering

Drag any category tab left or right to reorder. The order is saved automatically.

Sorting expansions

Click the sort button in the expansion list header to sort A→Z by Trigger or by Name.

Global Variables

Store your personal details once — name, email, phone, company — and Trigr inserts them silently into any expansion that uses them. No popup, no interruption.

📷
Screenshot
SCREENSHOT 16
My Details tab open in Text Expansions panel — fields showing Full Name, Email, Phone, Company filled in, Save button visible

Setting up your details

  1. Go to Text Expansions and click the My Details tab in the category bar

  2. Fill in your Name, Email, Phone, Company and any custom fields

  3. Click Save

Using variables in expansions

When creating or editing an expansion, click Insert in the toolbar and choose a variable from the dropdown. It appears as a token in the editor.

VariableInserts
{{name}}Your full name
{{email}}Your email address
{{phone}}Your phone number
{{company}}Your company name
Silent substitution

Unlike fill-in fields which show a popup, Global Variables substitute silently with no interruption. If a variable has no value stored, Trigr falls back to a fill-in prompt.

Instant trigger

By default expansions fire when you press Space after the trigger word. Instant mode fires the moment the last character is typed — no Space needed.

Enable per expansion using the ⚡ Instant toggle below the trigger field when creating or editing. The expansion list shows a lightning bolt icon on instant-mode items.

Use with care

Instant mode can fire unexpectedly if your trigger word appears inside longer words you type naturally. Use a distinctive prefix like / or . to avoid accidental matches.

Profiles

Profiles let you have different sets of hotkeys for different purposes. Default is always active; app-linked profiles take over automatically when that app is in focus.

  • Double-click a profile tab to rename it (except Default)
  • Right-click a profile tab — rename, duplicate or delete
  • Drag profile tabs to reorder them
  • Click + to create a new profile
Moving assignments between profiles

Open any assignment and click Copy to Profile or Move to Profile in the footer to transfer it without recreating from scratch.

App-specific profiles

Link a profile to an application — Trigr auto-switches when that app comes into focus, and switches back when you leave it.

📷
Screenshot
SCREENSHOT 12
App-specific profile — BricsCAD profile active, Bare Keys layer selected, bare key P assigned with PLINE command in right panel

  1. Click + to create a new profile and name it — e.g. "BricsCAD"

  2. In the profile settings, use Link to App and browse to the application's .exe file

  3. A coloured dot appears on the profile tab confirming it is linked

  4. Add hotkey assignments to this profile — they only fire when that app is in focus

Bare key assignments

App-specific profiles unlock a Bare tab in the modifier bar. Bare key hotkeys have no modifier — they fire from a single keypress but only when the linked app is in the foreground.

CAD programs

Use bare keys for single-letter shortcuts that only activate inside the CAD window — they won't interfere with typing in emails or other apps.

Auto-switching pauses while editing

While Trigr is in focus, profile auto-switching is paused. This lets you configure your CAD profile without it switching away mid-edit.

Autocorrect

Automatically fix common typos as you type, in any application.

In the Text Expansions panel, click the TEXT EXPANSIONS label in the top-left — it toggles between Text Expansions view and Autocorrect view.

Built-in corrections

Toggle the built-in library to enable around 50 common English typo corrections at once:

You typeCorrected to
tehthe
recievereceive
occuredoccurred
seperateseparate
definatelydefinitely
imI'm

Custom corrections

Click + Add in the Autocorrect view. Enter the typo on the left and the correction on the right. Corrections fire on Space, same as text expansions.

Settings

Access settings via the cog icon ⚙ in the top-right of the title bar.

Compatibility

If text output is not working correctly in a specific application, change the input method:

MethodBest for
Direct KeystrokesCAD software, BricsCAD, engineering tools. Default. Works out of the box.
Clipboard (Shift+Insert)Apps that block Ctrl+V but accept Shift+Insert
Clipboard (Ctrl+V)Standard applications — fastest method
SendInput APIMaximum compatibility — injects at the Windows level

The Pre-execution delay slider (0–500ms) sets how long Trigr waits after a hotkey fires before sending output. Increase if an application needs more time to be ready to receive input.

Global Pause Toggle

Assign a modifier combo to instantly pause all Trigr hotkeys and expansions. Configured in Settings — see the Global Pause page for full details.

Quick Search

Press Ctrl+Space (default) from anywhere to open a floating search panel across all your macros and expansions. Change the hotkey in Settings → Quick Search.

Backup & Restore

Use Export Config to save all your assignments, profiles and expansions as a JSON backup file. Use Import Config to restore from backup. Always export before reinstalling or making large structural changes.

Do not store passwords

Avoid storing passwords or credentials as text expansions or macro text. Use a dedicated password manager such as Bitwarden or 1Password. All Trigr data is stored locally on your device only — nothing is ever transmitted to any external server.

Global Pause

Instantly pause all of Trigr with a single key combo — no hotkeys fire, no expansions trigger, all keypresses pass through to your apps normally. Press the same combo again to resume.

Setting up

  1. Go to Settings → Global Pause

  2. Click Assign and press your desired combo — e.g. Ctrl+Alt+Q

  3. The combo is saved and registered immediately

Modifier required

The pause hotkey must include at least one modifier key — Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Win. Bare keys are not accepted to prevent accidental pausing while typing.

Conflict detection

If the combo is already used by a hotkey in any profile, Trigr shows an amber warning and blocks saving. Choose a combo not used elsewhere.

Visual indicators when paused

ElementPaused state
Title barShows PAUSED in amber instead of green ACTIVE
System tray iconSwaps to paused variant with pause symbol
Taskbar buttonShows white ⏸ overlay badge
Tray tooltipShows "Trigr — Paused"
All hotkeys & expansionsStop firing — keypresses pass through normally
Always resumable

The pause hotkey stays registered at all times — even while paused — so you can always unpause regardless of what app is in focus. Trigr always starts unpaused on launch.

Quick Search

A floating search panel that surfaces any hotkey, macro or expansion instantly — without opening the main Trigr window.

Opening Quick Search

Press Win+Space from anywhere — even when Trigr's window is closed — to open the panel. Start typing immediately; results filter live across all profiles and categories.

What you can do

ActionHow
Fire a macro or expansionSelect the result, press Enter
Open the assignment in the editorSelect the result, press Tab
Navigate resultsArrow keys ↑ ↓
Close without firingPress Esc or click outside

Changing the hotkey

Go to Settings → Quick Search and click the hotkey field. Press your desired combination. The default is Ctrl+Space. Change it to a function key or another combination if it conflicts with your system.

Fire without opening Trigr

Quick Search lets you trigger any macro or expansion even if the Trigr window has never been opened in that session. As long as the tray icon is running, the search panel works.

Troubleshooting

Common issues and how to fix them. If a problem persists, check the status bar — both Listener and Executor chips should be green.

Macros not firing

SymptomLikely causeFix
Nothing happens at allEngine not runningCheck status bar — if Listener/Executor chips are not shown or grey, restart Trigr via the tray icon
Macro fires in some apps but not othersApp intercepts the hotkey before TrigrChange the hotkey to a different combination less likely to conflict
Correct key pressed but wrong macro firesWrong profile activeCheck the profile tab — the active one is highlighted amber. Auto-switching may have changed it.
Hotkey fires but nothing is typedInput method mismatchGo to Settings → Compatibility and switch to Direct Keystrokes or SendInput API

Text output problems

SymptomFix
Text appears with wrong characters or missing lettersTry Clipboard (Ctrl+V) mode — avoids keystroke translation issues
Text pasted in wrong field or wrong windowIncrease the Pre-execution delay in Settings (try 150–300ms)
CAD app ignores typed textUse Direct Keystrokes mode — required by most CAD applications including BricsCAD
Rich text formatting lostThe target app may not support rich text via clipboard. Use the "Paste as plain text" toggle on the expansion.

Text expansions not triggering

SymptomFix
Trigger word typed + Space but nothing happensCheck that Text Expansions are enabled — look for the "TEXT EXPANSIONS" label below the keyboard. It should not be greyed out.
Only fires sometimesMake sure you are pressing Space after the final character with no pause.
Fires inside words unexpectedlyYour trigger word appears as part of longer words. Use a distinctive prefix like / or .
Dynamic date field shows wrong dateVerify system date/time is correct in Windows Settings

Profile auto-switching not working

  1. Confirm the profile has a coloured dot — this indicates it is linked to an app

  2. In profile settings, click Link to App and verify the correct .exe path is set

  3. Make sure Trigr itself is not in focus — auto-switching pauses while you are editing inside Trigr

  4. Some apps launch through launchers or wrappers — link to the actual process .exe, not a shortcut

Status bar reference

Status bar stateMeaning
● Macros Active with green Listener + Executor chipsEverything running normally
Listener chip missing or greyKeyboard/mouse hook not registered — restart Trigr
Executor chip missing or greyOutput engine not ready — restart Trigr, check antivirus isn't blocking it
Macros PausedYou have manually paused via tray icon or keyboard shortcut — click tray icon to resume
Antivirus false positives

Some antivirus tools flag keyboard hook software as suspicious. If Trigr stops working after an antivirus scan, add the Trigr install folder to your AV exclusions list. Trigr runs entirely offline — nothing is sent to any server.

Run as Administrator

A small number of applications — primarily administrative tools and some games — run at elevated privilege and block input from non-elevated processes. If macros don't fire in a specific app, try right-clicking the Trigr tray icon and selecting Restart as Administrator.
